prompt: What does membersihkan mean?
opt_a_why: Correct - bersih plus -kan makes the causative make clean.
opt_b_why: That is just the adjective bersih.
opt_c_why: Cleanliness is kebersihan.
opt_d_why: The -kan suffix does not make a doer noun.
explanation: -kan often makes a causative verb: membersihkan means to make something clean.

prompt: One main job of the -kan suffix is to make a verb...
opt_a_why: Correct - -kan can add a make-it-happen meaning.
opt_b_why: Malay verbs have no tense.
opt_c_why: -kan does not form questions.
opt_d_why: -kan does not mark number.
explanation: -kan makes verbs causative (make something) or benefactive (do it for someone).

prompt: Which verb means to explain (to make clear), from the root jelas?
opt_a_why: Correct - jelas plus me- and -kan.
opt_b_why: The ber- prefix does not fit here.
opt_c_why: ter- means most or accidental.
opt_d_why: The -i suffix would point to a place.
explanation: jelas plus -kan gives menjelaskan, to make clear.

prompt: Make the verb to buy for someone, from the root beli
accepted: membelikan
explanation: beli plus me- and -kan gives membelikan - to buy for someone.

prompt: The -i suffix usually signals that the object is...
opt_a_why: Correct - -i often points to a location.
opt_b_why: It is not limited to people.
opt_c_why: Malay has no tense suffixes.
opt_d_why: -i does not mark number.
explanation: -i tends to mark a place or surface, as in menduduki (to occupy).

prompt: Which verb means to visit someone, from the root kunjung?
opt_a_why: Correct - kunjung plus me- and -i.
opt_b_why: The -kan suffix would change the meaning.
opt_c_why: berkunjung is intransitive and cannot take an object directly.
opt_d_why: ter- does not fit here.
explanation: mengunjungi means to visit a person or place.

prompt: From the root masuk, which verb means to enter a place?
opt_a_why: Correct - memasuki acts on the place you enter.
opt_b_why: memasukkan means to put something in.
opt_c_why: This is not a real verb.
opt_d_why: termasuk means included.
explanation: memasuki means to enter; memasukkan means to put something in.

prompt: The rule of thumb is: -kan moves something, and -i...
opt_a_why: Correct - -i typically acts on a location or surface.
opt_b_why: Neither suffix forms questions.
opt_c_why: Malay has no tense.
opt_d_why: Neither suffix marks number.
explanation: -kan moves an object; -i acts on a place.

prompt: The verb terjatuh suggests the fall was...
opt_a_why: Correct - ter- here means it happened unintentionally.
opt_b_why: ter- signals the opposite of planned.
opt_c_why: ter- does not mean repeated.
opt_d_why: ter- does not mark tense.
explanation: ter- can mark an accidental action: terjatuh means fell by accident.

prompt: What does terbesar mean?
opt_a_why: Correct - ter- on an adjective makes a superlative.
opt_b_why: That would be an accidental ter- verb.
opt_c_why: ter- does not mark the future.
opt_d_why: ter- does not mark tense.
explanation: ter- plus an adjective means the most: terbesar is biggest.

prompt: Which is NOT a job of the ter- prefix?
opt_a_why: Correct - ter- never marks tense; Malay has no tenses.
opt_b_why: ter- does do this, as in terlupa.
opt_c_why: ter- does do this, as in terbaik.
opt_d_why: ter- does do this, as in tidak terangkat.
explanation: ter- marks accidental actions, superlatives and ability - but never tense.

prompt: Make the accidental verb meaning I forgot, using ter- with the root lupa
accepted: terlupa|saya terlupa
explanation: lupa plus ter- gives terlupa - forgot accidentally.

prompt: What does sudah mean?
opt_a_why: Correct - sudah marks a completed action.
opt_b_why: Not yet is belum.
opt_c_why: Will is akan.
opt_d_why: Still is masih.
explanation: sudah marks something already done.

prompt: What does belum mean?
opt_a_why: Correct - belum means not yet.
opt_b_why: Already is sudah.
opt_c_why: Now is sekarang.
opt_d_why: Always is sentiasa.
explanation: belum means the action has not happened yet.

prompt: What does Saya sedang makan mean?
opt_a_why: Correct - sedang marks an action in progress.
opt_b_why: That would use sudah.
opt_c_why: That would use akan.
opt_d_why: That would use tidak pernah.
explanation: sedang marks an action in progress, like English -ing.

prompt: The marker akan shows that an action is...
opt_a_why: Correct - akan means will or going to.
opt_b_why: A completed past uses sudah or telah.
opt_c_why: akan does not form questions.
opt_d_why: akan does not mean impossible.
explanation: akan marks the future: Saya akan pergi means I will go.

prompt: What does pernah mean?
opt_a_why: Correct - pernah marks past experience.
opt_b_why: Never is tidak pernah.
opt_c_why: Will is akan.
opt_d_why: Now is sekarang.
explanation: pernah marks experience: Saya pernah ke Melaka means I have been to Melaka.

prompt: What does kerana mean?
opt_a_why: Correct - kerana gives a reason.
opt_b_why: But is tetapi.
opt_c_why: Although is walaupun.
opt_d_why: So is jadi.
explanation: kerana (or sebab) means because.

prompt: What does walaupun mean?
opt_a_why: Correct - walaupun concedes a point.
opt_b_why: Because is kerana.
opt_c_why: And is dan.
opt_d_why: Or is atau.
explanation: walaupun (or meskipun) means although.

prompt: Which word means so that?
opt_a_why: Correct - supaya (or agar) means so that.
opt_b_why: kerana means because.
opt_c_why: tetapi means but.
opt_d_why: atau means or.
explanation: supaya and agar both mean so that, giving a purpose.

prompt: Which word means but?
opt_a_why: Correct - tetapi (spoken: tapi) means but.
opt_b_why: dan means and.
opt_c_why: atau means or.
opt_d_why: jadi means so.
explanation: tetapi means but; the colloquial form is tapi.

prompt: In orang yang tinggi, the word yang links the noun to...
opt_a_why: Correct - yang introduces a description of the noun.
opt_b_why: yang does not mark number.
opt_c_why: Malay has no tense.
opt_d_why: yang is not a location word.
explanation: yang links a noun to its description: orang yang tinggi means the tall person.

prompt: How do you say the book that I bought?
opt_a_why: Correct - noun, then yang, then the description.
opt_b_why: yang must come right after the noun buku.
opt_c_why: yang cannot start the phrase.
opt_d_why: The word order is wrong.
explanation: The pattern is noun plus yang plus description: buku yang saya beli.

prompt: What does yang merah mean?
opt_a_why: Correct - yang plus an adjective means the ... one.
opt_b_why: Very red would be sangat merah.
opt_c_why: Not red would be tidak merah.
opt_d_why: yang does not mark tense.
explanation: yang plus an adjective picks out the ... one: yang merah means the red one.

prompt: Translate to Malay: the person who is tall
accepted: orang yang tinggi
explanation: orang (person) plus yang plus tinggi (tall).

prompt: Which sentence is correct?
opt_a: Tolong hantarkan surat ini kepada pengarah.
opt_a_why: Correct - hantarkan takes a direct object (surat) and the benefactive sense fits.
opt_b: Tolong hantari surat ini kepada pengarah.
opt_b_why: hantari is wrong; -i would make the object a place, not the thing sent.
opt_c: Tolong berhantar surat ini kepada pengarah.
opt_c_why: berhantar is not a standard transitive verb here.
opt_d: Tolong terhantar surat ini kepada pengarah.
opt_d_why: terhantar means accidentally sent and cannot take a command.
explanation: For sending a thing to someone, hantarkan (with -kan) is the correct transitive form.

prompt: Compare: menaikkan vs menaiki. Which meaning is right?
opt_a: menaikkan harga means to raise the price; menaiki bas means to board a bus.
opt_a_why: Correct - -kan causes the rise; -i acts on the thing you get on.
opt_b: menaikkan harga means to board a price; menaiki bas means to raise a bus.
opt_b_why: The meanings are swapped.
opt_c: Both verbs mean exactly the same thing.
opt_c_why: They differ: -kan is causative, -i is locative.
opt_d: menaiki always means to lower something.
opt_d_why: menaiki never means to lower; the root naik means to go up.
explanation: menaikkan = make something go up (causative); menaiki = get on/ride something (locative).

prompt: Fill in the blank: Dia ___ wang itu di atas meja. (He put the money on the table)
accepted: meletakkan
explanation: letak plus -kan gives meletakkan, to place something somewhere.

prompt: Translate to Malay: Please explain this matter to me.
accepted: tolong jelaskan perkara ini kepada saya|sila jelaskan perkara ini kepada saya
explanation: jelaskan (jelas plus -kan) means to make clear; tolong or sila softens the request.

prompt: Translate to Malay: My mother cooked rice for me.
accepted: emak saya memasakkan saya nasi|ibu saya memasakkan saya nasi|emak saya memasak nasi untuk saya|ibu saya memasak nasi untuk saya
explanation: memasakkan uses the benefactive -kan: to cook for someone; memasak nasi untuk saya is also natural.

prompt: Arrange to mean He returned the book to the library: itu / mengembalikan / dia / ke / perpustakaan / buku
accepted: dia mengembalikan buku itu ke perpustakaan
explanation: mengembalikan (kembali plus -kan) means to return something; the order is subject, verb, object, place.

prompt: Which sentence contains a mistake?
opt_a: Dia membesari syarikat itu tahun lepas.
opt_a_why: Correct - this is the mistake; to enlarge a company you need membesarkan, not membesari.
opt_b: Dia membesarkan syarikat itu tahun lepas.
opt_b_why: This is correct - membesarkan means to expand or enlarge.
opt_c_why: This is correct - membersihkan means to clean something.
opt_d_why: This is correct - membetulkan means to correct something.
explanation: For to enlarge, the causative membesarkan is needed; membesari is not standard.

prompt: From the root tidur, what does menidurkan mean?
opt_a_why: Correct - the causative -kan makes someone sleep.
opt_b_why: To sleep is just tidur.
opt_c_why: Sleepy is mengantuk.
opt_d_why: A bed is katil.
explanation: menidurkan means to make someone sleep, a causative use of -kan.

prompt: Fill in the blank: Jurulatih itu ___ pemain baharu kepada pasukan. (introduced the new player to the team)
accepted: memperkenalkan
explanation: memperkenalkan means to introduce; it is built from kenal with memper- and -kan.

prompt: Which sentence is correct?
opt_a_why: Correct - menandatangani (to sign) takes the document as object with -i.
opt_b_why: menandatangankan is not the standard form for signing a document.
opt_c_why: bertandatangan cannot take a direct object like this.
opt_d_why: tertandatangan would mean accidentally signed and is wrong here.
explanation: To sign a document, the standard verb is menandatangani, using -i.

prompt: Compare menanam and menanami. Which is right?
opt_a: menanami kebun means to plant the garden (with crops); menanam pokok means to plant a tree.
opt_a_why: Correct - -i marks the place planted; the bare verb takes the thing planted.
opt_b: menanami pokok means to plant a tree.
opt_b_why: menanami needs a place as object, not the plant itself.
opt_c: menanam kebun is the only correct phrase.
opt_c_why: menanam takes the thing planted; for the place, menanami is used.
opt_d: Both verbs always mean to dig.
opt_d_why: The root tanam means to plant, not to dig.
explanation: menanam takes the plant; menanami takes the place that gets planted.

prompt: Fill in the blank: Ramai pelancong ___ pulau itu setiap tahun. (visit that island)
accepted: mengunjungi
explanation: mengunjungi (kunjung plus -i) means to visit a place or person.

prompt: Translate to Malay: The students obey the teacher.
accepted: pelajar mematuhi guru itu|murid mematuhi guru itu|para pelajar mematuhi guru itu
explanation: mematuhi (patuh plus -i) means to obey or comply with.

prompt: Translate to Malay: We must love our country.
accepted: kita mesti mencintai negara kita|kita perlu mencintai negara kita|kita harus mencintai negara kita
explanation: mencintai (cinta plus -i) means to love; mesti, perlu or harus all express must.

prompt: Arrange to mean The committee approached the difficult problem: itu / mendekati / jawatankuasa / sukar / masalah / yang
accepted: jawatankuasa itu mendekati masalah yang sukar
explanation: mendekati (dekat plus -i) means to approach; -i marks the thing approached.

prompt: Which sentence contains a mistake?
opt_a: Air sungai itu menggenangi sawah padi.
opt_a_why: This is correct - menggenangi means to flood a place, taking the place as object.
opt_b_why: This is correct - membasahi means to wet something.
opt_c: Dia memasukkan kunci ke dalam laci.
opt_c_why: This is correct - memasukkan means to put something in.
opt_d: Dia memasuki buku ke dalam beg.
opt_d_why: Correct - this is the mistake; to put a book in, use memasukkan, not memasuki.
explanation: memasuki means to enter a place; to put something in, the verb is memasukkan.

prompt: From the root duduk, what does menduduki mean?
opt_a: to occupy (a seat, position or place)
opt_a_why: Correct - menduduki acts on the place or position occupied.
opt_b_why: That would be mendudukkan.
opt_c_why: To stand is berdiri.
opt_d_why: A chair is kerusi.
explanation: menduduki means to occupy a place or position; mendudukkan means to seat someone.

prompt: Which sentence is correct?
opt_a: Saya tidak terangkat kotak yang berat itu.
opt_a_why: Correct - ter- with tidak expresses inability: I could not lift it.
opt_b: Saya tidak mengangkat boleh kotak itu.
opt_b_why: The word order of boleh is wrong and awkward.
opt_c_why: The -kan suffix does not combine with the ter- ability sense here.
opt_d: Saya tidak berangkat kotak yang berat itu.
opt_d_why: berangkat means to depart, not to lift.
explanation: tidak ter- plus a root expresses inability: tidak terangkat means cannot be lifted.

prompt: In Pintu itu terbuka, what does terbuka most likely mean?
opt_a_why: Correct - ter- here marks a state, often with no clear agent: the door is open.
opt_b_why: ter- never marks the future.
opt_c_why: To close is menutup; terbuka relates to open.
opt_d_why: ter- does not form a doer noun.
explanation: ter- can mark a resultant state: terbuka means is (already) open.

prompt: Fill in the blank: Dia ___ kerana terpijak lantai yang licin, lalu jatuh. (slipped accidentally)
accepted: tergelincir
explanation: tergelincir uses ter- for an accidental action: slipped without meaning to.

prompt: Translate to Malay: This is the best restaurant in town.
accepted: ini restoran terbaik di bandar|ini ialah restoran terbaik di bandar|ini adalah restoran terbaik di bandar
explanation: terbaik uses ter- plus baik to form the superlative best.

prompt: Translate to Malay: I accidentally stepped on the cat.
accepted: saya terpijak kucing itu|saya terpijak kucing
explanation: terpijak uses ter- to mark an accidental action: stepped on without intending to.

prompt: Which sentence contains a mistake?
opt_a: Gunung itu ialah yang tertinggi di negara ini.
opt_a_why: This is correct - tertinggi is the superlative highest.
opt_b_why: This is correct - terlupa means forgot accidentally.
opt_c_why: Correct - this is the mistake; the future is shown by akan, not ter-.
opt_d_why: This is correct - terjatuh means fell accidentally.
explanation: ter- never marks the future; a planned future trip uses akan pergi.

prompt: Arrange to mean The novel was written by a famous author: terkenal / itu / oleh / novel / penulis / ditulis
accepted: novel itu ditulis oleh penulis terkenal
explanation: terkenal (ter- plus kenal) means famous; ditulis is the passive of tulis.

prompt: What is the difference between terbaik and paling baik?
opt_a: They mean the same thing - both are the superlative best
opt_a_why: Correct - terbaik and paling baik are interchangeable ways to say best.
opt_b_why: terbaik means best; the root is baik (good).
opt_c_why: paling marks the superlative, not an accident.
opt_d: terbaik is the future of baik
opt_d_why: ter- does not mark tense.
explanation: The superlative can be formed with ter- (terbaik) or with paling (paling baik).

prompt: Fill in the blank: Buku ini ___ tebal antara semua buku di rak ini. (the thickest)
accepted: tertebal
explanation: tertebal uses ter- plus tebal to form the superlative thickest.

prompt: What is the key difference between sudah and pernah?
opt_a: sudah marks a completed action; pernah marks having the experience at least once.
opt_a_why: Correct - sudah says it is done; pernah says it has happened before.
opt_b: They mean exactly the same thing.
opt_b_why: They differ: sudah is completion, pernah is experience.
opt_c_why: The future is akan; pernah is about past experience.
opt_d_why: Not yet is belum; sudah means already.
explanation: sudah = already done; pernah = have ever done it (experience).

prompt: Which sentence is correct for I have already eaten?
opt_a_why: Correct - sudah marks the action as completed.
opt_b_why: akan means will eat, the future, not already.
opt_c_why: belum means have not eaten yet.
opt_d_why: sedang means am eating right now.
explanation: sudah marks a completed action: Saya sudah makan means I have already eaten.

prompt: Which sentence contains a mistake?
opt_a: Dia masih belum tiba di pejabat.
opt_a_why: This is correct - masih belum means still has not yet.
opt_b_why: This is correct - sudah pernah emphasises past experience naturally.
opt_c_why: Correct - this is the mistake; akan (future) and sudah (completed) clash in this order.
opt_d_why: This is correct - sedang marks an action in progress.
explanation: akan and sudah contradict each other; you cannot mark something as future and already done.

prompt: Fill in the blank: Jangan risau, saya ___ menyiapkan laporan itu. (still in the process of)
accepted: sedang
explanation: sedang marks an action in progress: still doing it now.

prompt: Choose the best word: Saya ___ pergi ke Sabah, jadi saya tidak tahu tempat itu.
opt_a_why: Correct - tidak pernah means never (no such experience), which fits the reason given.
opt_b_why: sudah means already, which contradicts not knowing the place.
opt_c_why: sedang means currently, which does not fit a lack of experience.
opt_d_why: akan means will, the future, which does not explain present unfamiliarity.
explanation: tidak pernah means never have; it explains why the speaker does not know the place.

prompt: What is the difference between sudah and telah?
opt_a: They both mark completion; telah is more formal and common in writing.
opt_a_why: Correct - telah and sudah both mean already, but telah is more formal.
opt_b_why: telah marks completion, not the future.
opt_c_why: Never is tidak pernah; sudah means already.
opt_d_why: Not yet is belum.
explanation: sudah and telah both mark completion; telah sounds more formal and is common in writing.

prompt: Translate to Malay: I have never been to Sarawak.
accepted: saya tidak pernah ke sarawak|saya belum pernah ke sarawak
explanation: tidak pernah means never; belum pernah (not yet ever) is also natural.

prompt: Translate to Malay: They are still waiting for the bus.
accepted: mereka masih menunggu bas|mereka masih menunggu bas itu
explanation: masih means still, marking an action that continues.

prompt: Arrange to mean I will go to the market tomorrow: ke / akan / esok / saya / pergi / pasar
accepted: saya akan pergi ke pasar esok
explanation: akan marks the future; the time word esok can sit at the end.

prompt: Fill in the blank: Bas itu ___ bertolak, jadi kita perlu menunggu bas seterusnya. (already, completed)
accepted: sudah|telah
explanation: sudah or telah marks the completed action: the bus has already departed.

prompt: Which sentence is correct for She has not arrived yet?
opt_a_why: Correct - belum means not yet, fitting an action that has not happened.
opt_b_why: tidak tiba means does not arrive, not the not yet meaning.
opt_c_why: sudah tiba means has already arrived, the opposite.
opt_d_why: akan tiba means will arrive, the future.
explanation: belum means not yet; tidak is a plain negative without the not yet sense.

prompt: Choose the correct word: ___ hujan lebat, mereka tetap pergi memancing.
opt_a_why: Correct - walaupun (although) fits because going fishing is unexpected given the rain.
opt_b_why: kerana (because) would wrongly say the rain caused them to go.
opt_c_why: supaya (so that) marks a purpose, not a contrast.
opt_d_why: atau (or) does not link a contrast.
explanation: walaupun introduces a concession: although it rained, they still went.

prompt: Choose the correct word: Dia belajar bersungguh-sungguh ___ lulus peperiksaan.
opt_a_why: Correct - supaya (so that) gives the purpose of studying hard.
opt_b_why: tetapi (but) marks a contrast, not a purpose.
opt_c_why: kerana would explain a cause, but here passing is the goal.
opt_d_why: walaupun (although) marks a concession, not a purpose.
explanation: supaya marks purpose: he studied hard so that he would pass.

prompt: Which sentence contains a mistake?
opt_a: Dia tidak datang kerana dia sakit.
opt_a_why: This is correct - kerana gives the reason for not coming.
opt_b: Walaupun dia kaya, dia sangat berjimat.
opt_b_why: This is correct - walaupun introduces a concession.
opt_c: Saya suka kopi kerana saya tidak suka teh.
opt_c_why: This is acceptable - kerana states a reason.
opt_d: Dia rajin walaupun dia mahu berjaya.
opt_d_why: Correct - this is the mistake; wanting to succeed is a reason, so kerana fits, not walaupun.
explanation: walaupun marks a contrast; a straightforward reason needs kerana or sebab.

prompt: Fill in the blank: Saya akan tunggu di sini ___ kamu kembali. (until)
accepted: sehingga|hingga
explanation: sehingga (or hingga) means until, marking the end point of the waiting.

prompt: Choose the correct word: ___ kamu rajin atau malas, kamu mesti menyiapkan tugasan ini.
opt_a_why: Correct - sama ada ... atau means whether ... or, fitting the two options.
opt_b_why: kerana introduces a single reason, not a whether-or choice.
opt_c_why: supaya marks purpose, not a choice.
opt_d_why: tetapi is a simple but and does not pair with atau here.
explanation: sama ada ... atau means whether ... or, presenting alternatives.

prompt: Translate to Malay: Although he is tired, he keeps working.
accepted: walaupun dia letih dia terus bekerja|meskipun dia letih dia terus bekerja|walaupun dia penat dia terus bekerja
explanation: walaupun (or meskipun) means although; terus bekerja means keeps working.

prompt: Translate to Malay: I stayed at home because it was raining.
accepted: saya tinggal di rumah kerana hujan|saya duduk di rumah kerana hujan|saya tinggal di rumah sebab hujan
explanation: kerana (or sebab) introduces the reason for staying home.

prompt: Arrange to mean He ran fast so that he would not be late: lewat / supaya / dia / pantas / tidak / berlari
accepted: dia berlari pantas supaya tidak lewat
explanation: supaya marks the purpose; the clause after it states the goal.

prompt: Choose the correct word: Dia berasa gembira ___ sedih pada masa yang sama.
opt_a_why: Correct - dan (and) joins the two feelings that exist together.
opt_b_why: atau (or) would suggest only one feeling, not both at once.
opt_c_why: kerana introduces a reason, not a second feeling.
opt_d_why: walaupun marks a concession and would change the meaning.
explanation: dan simply joins two items: happy and sad at the same time.

prompt: Fill in the blank: Cuaca hari ini panas, ___ saya tetap mahu keluar berjoging. (but)
accepted: tetapi|namun|tapi
explanation: tetapi (or namun) marks the contrast between hot weather and still wanting to go out.

prompt: What is the difference between kerana and oleh sebab itu?
opt_a: kerana introduces the cause; oleh sebab itu introduces the result.
opt_a_why: Correct - kerana is followed by the reason; oleh sebab itu is followed by the consequence.
opt_b_why: They differ: one points to the cause, the other to the result.
opt_c_why: although is walaupun; oleh sebab itu means therefore.
opt_d_why: so that is supaya; kerana means because.
explanation: kerana = because (cause follows); oleh sebab itu = therefore (result follows).

prompt: How do you say the woman who is reading a book?
opt_a_why: Correct - yang follows the noun and introduces the descriptive clause.
opt_b_why: yang must come right after the noun, before sedang.
opt_c_why: yang cannot begin the phrase before the noun.
opt_d_why: yang is misplaced at the end.
explanation: The pattern is noun plus yang plus the clause: wanita yang sedang membaca buku.

prompt: Which sentence contains a mistake?
opt_a: Pelajar yang rajin itu mendapat hadiah.
opt_a_why: This is correct - yang links pelajar to its description rajin.
opt_b: Rumah yang besar itu milik datuk saya.
opt_b_why: This is correct - yang introduces the description besar.
opt_c_why: This is correct - yang links makanan to pedas.
opt_d_why: Correct - this is the mistake; the noun baju must come before yang, not after.
explanation: yang follows the noun it describes; the correct phrase is baju yang merah itu.

prompt: Fill in the blank: Lelaki ___ memandu kereta itu ialah jiran saya. (who)
accepted: yang
explanation: yang links lelaki to the clause describing him: the man who drives that car.

prompt: Translate to Malay: The food that we ate was delicious.
accepted: makanan yang kami makan sedap|makanan yang kami makan itu sedap
explanation: yang introduces the clause kami makan describing makanan.

prompt: Translate to Malay: I know the man who lives next door.
accepted: saya kenal lelaki yang tinggal di sebelah|saya mengenali lelaki yang tinggal di sebelah
explanation: yang links lelaki to the clause tinggal di sebelah.

prompt: Arrange to mean The student who won the prize is my friend: kawan / memenangi / pelajar / itu / saya / yang / hadiah / ialah
accepted: pelajar yang memenangi hadiah itu ialah kawan saya
explanation: yang introduces the clause memenangi hadiah describing pelajar.

prompt: Arrange to mean This is the house that my father built: ayah / dibina / rumah / ini / oleh / yang / saya
accepted: ini rumah yang dibina oleh ayah saya
explanation: yang introduces a passive clause dibina oleh ayah saya describing rumah.

prompt: In the phrase yang paling pandai, what does yang add?
opt_a: It picks out a specific one - the one who is cleverest
opt_a_why: Correct - yang nominalises the description, meaning the one that is.
opt_b_why: Malay has no tense, and yang does not mark it.
opt_c: It makes the phrase a question
opt_c_why: yang does not form questions.
opt_d_why: yang does not mark number.
explanation: yang plus a description picks out the one that is ...: yang paling pandai means the cleverest one.

prompt: Which sentence is correct for the keys that are on the table?
opt_a: kunci yang ada di atas meja
opt_a_why: Correct - yang follows kunci and introduces the location clause.
opt_b_why: yang is wrongly placed at the end of the phrase.
opt_c: yang kunci di atas meja ada
opt_c_why: yang cannot start the phrase, and the order is wrong.
opt_d: kunci ada yang di atas meja
opt_d_why: yang must come directly after the noun kunci.
explanation: The pattern is noun plus yang plus clause: kunci yang ada di atas meja.

prompt: Translate to Malay: The teacher asked the students to submit the assignment that was given last week.
accepted: guru itu meminta pelajar menghantar tugasan yang diberikan minggu lepas|guru meminta pelajar menghantar tugasan yang diberikan minggu lepas|guru itu menyuruh pelajar menghantar tugasan yang diberi minggu lepas
explanation: menghantar takes the assignment as a direct object; the yang clause diberikan minggu lepas describes tugasan.

prompt: Which sentence is correct in context: a manager telling staff to email the report to a client?
opt_a: Sila e-melkan laporan itu kepada pelanggan.
opt_a_why: Correct - e-melkan takes the report as object and -kan carries the send-to sense.
opt_b: Sila e-meli laporan itu kepada pelanggan.
opt_b_why: -i would treat the report as a place receiving something, which is wrong here.
opt_c: Sila e-mel laporan itu terhadap pelanggan.
opt_c_why: terhadap (towards/regarding) does not mark a recipient; kepada is needed.
opt_d: Sila tere-mel laporan itu kepada pelanggan.
opt_d_why: ter- marks an accidental action and cannot form a polite command.
explanation: To send a thing to a recipient, -kan is used: e-melkan ... kepada pelanggan.

prompt: Spot the error: which sentence is wrong?
opt_a: Dia menjelaskan masalah itu kepada kami.
opt_a_why: This is correct - menjelaskan (make clear) takes masalah as object.
opt_b: Pekerja itu menaikkan bendera setiap pagi.
opt_b_why: This is correct - menaikkan means to raise something.
opt_c: Saya membersihi bilik tidur saya semalam.
opt_c_why: Correct - this is the error; to clean a room you use membersihkan, not membersihi.
opt_d: Mereka mengembalikan buku itu ke perpustakaan.
opt_d_why: This is correct - mengembalikan means to return something.
explanation: membersihkan (clean something) uses -kan; membersihi is not the standard verb here.

prompt: Translate to Malay: He explained to us why the project that we planned had failed.
accepted: dia menjelaskan kepada kami mengapa projek yang kami rancang itu gagal|dia menjelaskan kepada kami kenapa projek yang kami rancang itu gagal|dia menjelaskan kepada kami sebab projek yang kami rancang gagal
explanation: menjelaskan uses -kan; the nested yang clause kami rancang describes projek before the main verb gagal.

prompt: Choose the verb that fits: Pemandu itu ___ penumpang di hadapan stesen.
opt_a_why: Correct - menurunkan (turun plus -kan) means to drop someone off; the passenger is what moves.
opt_b_why: menuruni means to go down something, like menuruni tangga, not to drop a passenger.
opt_c_why: berturun is not a standard transitive verb for this meaning.
opt_d_why: terturun would imply an accidental fall and does not fit a deliberate drop-off.
explanation: menurunkan moves an object down or off; -i would mark a surface being descended.

prompt: Translate to Malay: The committee that you lead must comply with the new rules.
accepted: jawatankuasa yang anda pimpin mesti mematuhi peraturan baharu|jawatankuasa yang kamu pimpin mesti mematuhi peraturan baru|jawatankuasa yang anda pimpin perlu mematuhi peraturan baharu
explanation: mematuhi (patuh plus -i) takes peraturan as object; the yang clause anda pimpin describes jawatankuasa.

prompt: -kan vs -i in context: a flood covering farmland. Which verb is correct?
opt_a: Banjir itu menggenangi kawasan sawah selama tiga hari.
opt_a_why: Correct - menggenangi takes the place flooded as its object; -i marks the affected location.
opt_b: Banjir itu menggenangkan kawasan sawah selama tiga hari.
opt_b_why: menggenangkan would mean to cause something to pool, not to flood a place.
opt_c: Banjir itu bergenang kawasan sawah selama tiga hari.
opt_c_why: bergenang is intransitive and cannot take kawasan sawah as a direct object.
opt_d: Banjir itu tergenang kawasan sawah selama tiga hari.
opt_d_why: tergenang describes water being pooled, not an action on the farmland.
explanation: menggenangi uses -i because the farmland is the surface acted upon by the flood.

prompt: Spot the error: which sentence is wrong?
opt_a: Pengawal itu memasuki bangunan melalui pintu belakang.
opt_a_why: This is correct - memasuki (enter) takes the building as the place entered.
opt_b: Dia menghadiahkan sebuah jam kepada ibunya.
opt_b_why: This is correct - menghadiahkan means to give something as a gift.
opt_c: Saya menasihatkan adik saya supaya belajar bersungguh-sungguh.
opt_c_why: Correct - this is the error; you advise a person, so menasihati is correct, not menasihatkan.
opt_d: Mereka menghormati guru yang mengajar mereka.
opt_d_why: This is correct - menghormati (respect) takes the teacher as object with -i.
explanation: menasihati uses -i because the person is advised; menasihatkan would push a piece of advice.

prompt: Translate to Malay: Many tourists visit the old temple that stands near the river.
accepted: ramai pelancong mengunjungi kuil lama yang terletak berhampiran sungai|ramai pelancong mengunjungi kuil lama yang terletak dekat sungai|ramai pelancong melawati kuil lama yang berdiri berhampiran sungai
explanation: mengunjungi uses -i for the place visited; the yang clause describes kuil lama with terletak (is located).

prompt: Which pair correctly contrasts -kan and -i?
opt_a: menghadiahkan jam means to give a watch as a gift; menghadiahi seseorang means to present someone with a gift.
opt_a_why: Correct - -kan moves the gift; -i marks the person who receives it.
opt_b: menghadiahkan and menghadiahi mean exactly the same thing.
opt_b_why: They differ: -kan focuses on the gift moved, -i on the person honoured.
opt_c: menghadiahi jam means to give a watch as a gift.
opt_c_why: menghadiahi takes the person, not the gift, as its object.
opt_d: menghadiahkan seseorang means to receive a gift from someone.
opt_d_why: menghadiahkan does not mean to receive; the root hadiah means gift, not receiving.
explanation: With -kan the gift is the object; with -i the recipient is the object.

prompt: Translate to Malay: This is the most expensive book that I have ever bought.
accepted: ini buku termahal yang pernah saya beli|ini ialah buku termahal yang pernah saya beli|ini adalah buku termahal yang pernah saya beli
explanation: termahal uses ter- for the superlative; the yang clause with pernah marks past experience.

prompt: Spot the error: which sentence is wrong?
opt_a: Kunci itu terjatuh ke dalam longkang semasa dia berlari.
opt_a_why: This is correct - terjatuh marks an accidental fall.
opt_b: Dia terbaca surat yang bukan miliknya.
opt_b_why: This is correct - terbaca means read accidentally or happened to read.
opt_c: Minggu depan mereka terpergi ke Langkawi untuk bercuti.
opt_c_why: Correct - this is the error; a planned future trip uses akan pergi, not ter-.
opt_d: Pencuri itu tertangkap oleh polis pada malam itu.
opt_d_why: This is correct - tertangkap means got caught.
explanation: ter- can mark accidental actions or results, but it never marks a planned future.

prompt: Choose the correct sentence: a worker says the box was too heavy to lift.
opt_a: Kotak itu terlalu berat sehingga tidak terangkat oleh saya.
opt_a_why: Correct - tidak terangkat expresses inability; the box could not be lifted.
opt_b: Kotak itu terlalu berat sehingga tidak mengangkatkan saya.
opt_b_why: mengangkatkan reverses the roles and is ungrammatical here.
opt_c: Kotak itu terlalu berat sehingga saya tidak terangkatkan.
opt_c_why: -kan does not combine with the ter- inability sense in this structure.
opt_d: Kotak itu terlalu berat sehingga saya berangkat.
opt_d_why: berangkat means to depart, not to lift.
explanation: tidak ter- plus a root expresses inability: tidak terangkat means could not be lifted.

prompt: Translate to Malay: She accidentally deleted the file that contained the report.
accepted: dia terpadam fail yang mengandungi laporan itu|dia terpadam fail yang ada laporan itu|dia tersilap memadam fail yang mengandungi laporan itu
explanation: terpadam uses ter- for an accidental action; the yang clause describes which file.

prompt: What does ter- mean in: Dia terdiam apabila mendengar berita itu?
opt_a: He fell silent suddenly and involuntarily.
opt_a_why: Correct - ter- here marks a sudden, unintended change of state: he became silent.
opt_b_why: ter- never marks the future.
opt_c: He is the most silent person.
opt_c_why: A superlative would need an adjective like paling pendiam, not this verb.
opt_d_why: ter- does not make a causative; that would be mendiamkan.
explanation: ter- can mark a sudden involuntary reaction: terdiam means to fall silent unexpectedly.

prompt: Translate to Malay: I have not finished the work because I am still waiting for the documents.
accepted: saya belum selesai kerja itu kerana saya masih menunggu dokumen|saya belum habis kerja itu kerana saya masih menunggu dokumen itu|saya belum siap kerja itu kerana masih menunggu dokumen
explanation: belum marks not yet; masih marks an ongoing action; kerana links the reason.

prompt: Spot the error: which sentence is wrong?
opt_a: Mereka sudah pernah mengunjungi muzium itu.
opt_a_why: This is correct - sudah pernah naturally emphasises a completed past experience.
opt_b: Dia masih belum menerima jawapan daripada syarikat itu.
opt_b_why: This is correct - masih belum means still has not yet.
opt_c: Esok kami akan sudah tiba di Pulau Pinang.
opt_c_why: Correct - this is the error; akan (future) and sudah (completed) contradict each other.
opt_d: Saya sedang membaca buku yang kamu cadangkan.
opt_d_why: This is correct - sedang marks an action in progress.
explanation: akan marks the future and sudah marks completion, so they cannot describe the same action together.

prompt: Choose the best combination: Walaupun dia ___ berlatih keras, dia ___ memenangi pertandingan itu.
opt_a_why: Correct - sudah (already trained) contrasts with belum (not yet won), and walaupun marks the concession.
opt_b_why: This says he has not trained but has already won, which makes no sense with walaupun.
opt_c_why: akan (will) and sudah clash, and the time relation breaks down.
opt_d_why: sedang ... akan removes the concessive contrast that walaupun requires.
explanation: walaupun needs a real contrast: sudah berlatih (already trained) versus belum menang (not yet won).

prompt: Translate to Malay: Although the test was difficult, she passed because she had studied hard.
accepted: walaupun ujian itu sukar dia lulus kerana dia telah belajar bersungguh-sungguh|meskipun ujian itu susah dia lulus kerana dia belajar bersungguh-sungguh|walaupun ujian itu sukar dia lulus kerana telah belajar dengan tekun
explanation: walaupun marks the concession and kerana introduces the reason in the same sentence.

prompt: Spot the error: which sentence is wrong?
opt_a: Dia berlari pantas supaya tidak ketinggalan bas.
opt_a_why: This is correct - supaya marks the purpose of running fast.
opt_b: Saya tidak keluar kerana hujan turun dengan lebat.
opt_b_why: This is correct - kerana introduces the reason for not going out.
opt_c: Walaupun dia mahu lulus, dia belajar setiap malam.
opt_c_why: Correct - this is the error; wanting to pass is the reason for studying, so kerana fits, not walaupun.
opt_d: Dia rajin, oleh sebab itu dia berjaya dalam peperiksaan.
opt_d_why: This is correct - oleh sebab itu introduces the result.
explanation: walaupun marks a contrast; a plain cause-and-effect link needs kerana, not walaupun.

prompt: Choose the correct word: Kami belum tahu ___ majlis itu akan diadakan atau ditangguhkan.
opt_a_why: Correct - sama ada ... atau means whether ... or, fitting the two unknown outcomes.
opt_b_why: walaupun marks a concession, not a whether-or choice.
opt_c_why: kerana introduces a reason, not an unresolved alternative.
opt_d_why: supaya marks purpose and does not pair with atau here.
explanation: sama ada ... atau presents two alternatives: whether the event will be held or postponed.

prompt: Translate to Malay: He worked overtime so that his family could live comfortably.
accepted: dia bekerja lebih masa supaya keluarganya dapat hidup dengan selesa|dia bekerja lebih masa supaya keluarganya boleh hidup selesa|dia bekerja lebih masa agar keluarganya dapat hidup dengan selesa
explanation: supaya (or agar) marks the purpose; the clause after it states the intended result.

prompt: Choose the linker that fits the logic: Dia tidak belajar langsung, ___ dia gagal dalam peperiksaan.
opt_a_why: Correct - oleh itu (therefore) introduces the result of not studying.
opt_b_why: walaupun marks a concession, but failing here is a consequence, not a contrast.
opt_c_why: sama ada introduces a whether-or choice, not a result.
opt_d_why: sebelum (before) marks time order, not a cause-and-effect result.
explanation: oleh itu introduces a consequence: he did not study, therefore he failed.

prompt: Translate to Malay: The man who bought the house that we sold is a doctor.
accepted: lelaki yang membeli rumah yang kami jual itu seorang doktor|lelaki yang membeli rumah yang kami jual ialah seorang doktor|lelaki yang membeli rumah yang kami jual itu ialah doktor
explanation: Two yang clauses are nested: yang membeli describes lelaki, and yang kami jual describes rumah.

prompt: Which sentence correctly uses a nested yang clause?
opt_a: Saya bertemu pelajar yang memenangi hadiah yang diberikan oleh sekolah.
opt_a_why: Correct - yang memenangi describes pelajar, and yang diberikan describes hadiah, each after its noun.
opt_b: Saya bertemu pelajar memenangi yang hadiah yang diberikan oleh sekolah.
opt_b_why: The first yang is misplaced; it must come right after pelajar.
opt_c: Saya bertemu yang pelajar memenangi hadiah yang diberikan oleh sekolah.
opt_c_why: yang cannot stand before the noun pelajar.
opt_d: Saya bertemu pelajar yang hadiah memenangi yang diberikan oleh sekolah.
opt_d_why: The words inside the first yang clause are out of order.
explanation: Each yang follows the noun it describes, so nested clauses keep the noun-yang-clause pattern.

prompt: Spot the error: which sentence is wrong?
opt_a: Buku yang saya pinjam daripada perpustakaan sudah saya kembalikan.
opt_a_why: This is correct - yang saya pinjam describes buku correctly.
opt_b: Pekerja yang rajin itu dinaikkan pangkat oleh majikannya.
opt_b_why: This is correct - yang rajin describes pekerja.
opt_c: Saya suka rumah yang besar dan yang selesa itu.
opt_c_why: This is acceptable - two yang phrases can describe the same noun rumah.
opt_d: Yang lelaki memandu kereta itu jiran saya.
opt_d_why: Correct - this is the error; the noun lelaki must come before yang, not after.
explanation: yang always follows the noun it describes; the correct phrase is lelaki yang memandu kereta itu.

prompt: Translate to Malay: The student who answered the question that the teacher asked received praise.
accepted: pelajar yang menjawab soalan yang ditanya oleh guru itu menerima pujian|pelajar yang menjawab soalan yang diajukan oleh guru itu menerima pujian|pelajar yang menjawab soalan yang ditanya guru itu mendapat pujian
explanation: yang menjawab describes pelajar; the nested yang ditanya oleh guru describes soalan.

prompt: Arrange to mean The car that was bought by the man who lives next door is new: tinggal / yang / di / itu / kereta / yang / dibeli / oleh / lelaki / sebelah / baharu
accepted: kereta yang dibeli oleh lelaki yang tinggal di sebelah itu baharu
explanation: The first yang clause describes kereta; the second yang clause describes lelaki, giving a nested structure.
